name: Gitea package registry (PyPI)
cost: Free and open-source (MIT) for self-hosting; the PyPI registry ships in the free OSS edition. The paid tiers wrap the same OSS core: Gitea Enterprise (self-hosted) is the "Most popular" tier at roughly $9.50-$19 per user/month with a 1-year commitment (about $114/seat/year at the low annual rate), adding SSO, audit logs, autoscaling runners, and SLA support. Managed Gitea Cloud (SaaS) is also paid but its per-seat price is not publicly listed on the pricing page (contact sales). Discounts noted for non-profits and small companies.
perf_notes: No PyPI-specific benchmarks published. The registry is a thin set of handlers inside the single Gitea Go binary; package metadata lives in Gitea's relational DB and file blobs are content-addressed and deduplicated on the configured storage backend (identical uploads share one blob). Throughput/latency are bounded by the chosen DB and storage backend rather than the registry layer itself.
scale_notes: Runs as a single binary for small deployments. HA/multi-node is documented (Gitea Enterprise HA guide) and achievable on OSS: stateless app nodes behind a load balancer with an external MySQL/Postgres cluster, Redis for sessions/cache/queues/global-lock, and shared object storage (S3/minio/Azure Blob) or a shared POSIX volume for package blobs. Caveats: the bleve code-search indexer is not HA-safe (use DB/Elasticsearch/Meilisearch) and logs are per-node, not shared.
summary: A PyPI registry built into the Gitea git forge that serves a PEP 503 simple index and accepts twine uploads, inheriting Gitea's users/orgs auth, RBAC, and storage backends rather than offering proxy/mirror or modern JSON-API features.
extras: PyPI registry routes are limited to exactly three handlers: GET /api/packages/{owner}/pypi/simple/{name} (PEP 503 HTML), GET .../files/{name}/{version}/{filename} (download), POST .../pypi (twine upload)

Simple-index anchors include #sha256=<digest> fragments and a conditional data-requires-python attribute, so pip/uv get hash-pinning and Requires-Python filtering; no data-yanked, data-dist-info-metadata, or PEP 629 api-version meta
Uploads must send a matching sha256_digest or are rejected ('hash mismatch'); name/version validated against PEP 426/440
No JSON simple index (PEP 691), PEP 700 extras, PEP 658 metadata files, PEP 592 yank, PEP 740 attestations, or OIDC trusted publishing - publishing is twine/flit/hatch POST over PAT/Basic auth only

All install clients (pip, uv, Poetry, PDM, Hatch, Pipenv) resolve from the standard PEP 503 index over HTTPS; all upload tools that speak the legacy POST API (twine, flit publish, hatch publish) can publish
Same name+version cannot be re-uploaded (immutable); you must delete the existing version first - no overwrite, no yank
Per-owner cleanup rules (age, keep-N, regex pattern; regex auto-anchored, case-insensitive) automate retention - but the cleanup job only loads the first 200 versions of each package
Blob deduplication: identical files across packages are stored once on disk
One of 24 package ecosystems in the same server (npm, Maven, NuGet, Cargo, Composer, RubyGems, Go, Conda, Container/OCI, Helm, Debian/RPM/Alpine/Arch, Generic, etc.)
Auth via personal access tokens with fine-grained 'package' read/write scopes, or username+password Basic auth; package visibility follows the owning user/org's public/private setting

Storage backends are global Gitea storage settings shared across all registries: STORAGE_TYPE = local, minio (S3-compatible), or azureblob (native Azure Blob); GCS only via its S3-interoperability endpoint through the minio driver
Audit logging and some SSO conveniences are positioned as Gitea Enterprise/Cloud paid features
No upstream PyPI proxy/mirror: clients must target a specific owner's index URL
